title: Calibrate baseline auto-initialization — remove dead end
description: When calibrationBaseline is absent from .forge/config.json, /forge:calibrate computes and writes the initial baseline (MASTER_INDEX.md hash + completed sprint IDs) instead of dead-ending with 'run /forge:init'. Mirrors init Phase 5 behavior.

plan.objective: Replace the dead-end in /forge:calibrate Step 2 with auto-initialization that computes and writes the calibration baseline when absent, mirroring init Phase 5 behavior.
key_changes: Replace Step 2 early-exit with auto-initialization logic in calibrate.md
Reuse existing baseline computation algorithm (MASTER_INDEX.md hash + completed sprint IDs)
Write baseline to .forge/config.json and report success instead of dead-ending
No schema changes — calibrationBaseline schema already exists

implementation.objective: Replace the dead-end in /forge:calibrate Step 2 with auto-initialization that computes and writes the calibration baseline when absent.
key_changes: Replaced Step 2 dead-end ('run /forge:init') with auto-initialization logic in forge/commands/calibrate.md
Auto-init mirrors init Phase 5/6-b: hash MASTER_INDEX.md, list completed sprints, write baseline to config
Updated health.md to recommend /forge:calibrate instead of /forge:init for missing baseline
Step 2 renamed from 'Validate prerequisites' to 'Establish or verify calibration baseline'
